name: beta_ge_two_of_condition
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem beta_ge_two_of_condition
    {beta C : Real}
    (hC : 2 <= C)
    (h1 : beta / 1 >= C) :
    2 <= beta := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Noncritical Belyi Maps, Lemma 2.1 arithmetic: if C >= 2 and beta/1 >= C, then beta >= 2. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: beta_gt_alpha_of_scale
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem beta_gt_alpha_of_scale
    {alpha beta C : Real}
    (hC : 2 <= C)
    (halpha_pos : 0 < alpha)
    (hscale : beta / alpha >= C) :
    alpha < beta := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 arithmetic: a scale ratio at least C >= 2 forces beta > alpha for positive alpha. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: ratio_sub_one_ge_ratio
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem ratio_sub_one_ge_ratio
    {alpha beta : Real}
    (halpha : 1 < alpha)
    (hbeta : alpha <= beta) :
    beta / alpha <= (beta - 1) / (alpha - 1) := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 arithmetic: shifting numerator and denominator by one increases the ratio under 1 < alpha <= beta. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: belyi_aux_ratio_lower_bound
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem belyi_aux_ratio_lower_bound
    {m n : Nat} {alpha beta : Real}
    (hm : 1 <= m)
    (hn : 1 <= n)
    (halpha : 1 < alpha)
    (hbeta : alpha <= beta) :
    (beta / alpha) <=
      (beta / alpha) ^ m * ((beta - 1) / (alpha - 1)) ^ n := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 arithmetic: lower-bound the auxiliary Belyi ratio by beta/alpha. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: belyi_aux_ratio_ge_quadratic
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem belyi_aux_ratio_ge_quadratic
    {m n : Nat} {alpha beta : Real}
    (hm : 1 <= m)
    (hn : 1 <= n)
    (halpha : 1 < alpha)
    (hbeta : alpha <= beta) :
    (beta / alpha) ^ 2 <=
      (beta / alpha) ^ m * ((beta - 1) / (alpha - 1)) ^ n := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 arithmetic: lower-bound the auxiliary Belyi ratio by the square of beta/alpha. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: belyi_aux_beta_gt_one
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem belyi_aux_beta_gt_one
    {m n : Nat} {beta : Real}
    (hm : 1 <= m)
    (hn : 1 <= n)
    (hbeta : 2 <= beta) :
    1 < NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n beta := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 arithmetic: the auxiliary polynomial is greater than one at beta >= 2. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: beta_le_belyi_aux_of_beta_ge_two
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem beta_le_belyi_aux_of_beta_ge_two
    {m n : Nat} {beta : Real}
    (hm : 1 <= m)
    (hn : 1 <= n)
    (hbeta : 2 <= beta) :
    beta <= NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n beta := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 arithmetic: for beta >= 2, the auxiliary polynomial beta^m*(beta-1)^n dominates beta. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: beta_le_two_mul_belyi_aux_of_beta_ge_two
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem beta_le_two_mul_belyi_aux_of_beta_ge_two
    {m n : Nat} {beta : Real}
    (hm : 1 <= m)
    (hn : 1 <= n)
    (hbeta : 2 <= beta) :
    beta <= 2 * NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n beta := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 odd-case arithmetic: for beta >= 2, twice the auxiliary value dominates beta. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: belyi_aux_pos_of_gt_one
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem belyi_aux_pos_of_gt_one
    {m n : Nat} {x : Real}
    (hx : 1 < x) :
    0 < NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n x := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 arithmetic: the auxiliary polynomial is positive for real x > 1. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: belyi_aux_beta_ge_four_mul_of_scale
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem belyi_aux_beta_ge_four_mul_of_scale
    {m n : Nat} {alpha beta C : Real}
    (hm : 1 <= m)
    (hn : 1 <= n)
    (hC : 2 <= C)
    (halpha : 1 < alpha)
    (hscale : beta / alpha >= C) :
    4 * NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n alpha <=
      NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n beta := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 property (c)/(d) arithmetic: if alpha > 1 and beta/alpha >= C >= 2, then f(beta) is at least four times f(alpha). Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: belyi_aux_beta_gt_of_scale
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem belyi_aux_beta_gt_of_scale
    {m n : Nat} {alpha beta C : Real}
    (hm : 1 <= m)
    (hn : 1 <= n)
    (hC : 2 <= C)
    (halpha : 1 < alpha)
    (hscale : beta / alpha >= C) :
    NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n alpha <
      NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n beta := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 property (c) arithmetic: the auxiliary polynomial strictly separates beta from positive points alpha > 1 under the scale hypothesis. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: belyi_aux_strict_mono_on_gt_one
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem belyi_aux_strict_mono_on_gt_one
    {m n : Nat} {alpha beta : Real}
    (hm : 1 <= m)
    (hn : 1 <= n)
    (halpha : 1 < alpha)
    (hbeta : alpha < beta) :
    NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n alpha <
      NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n beta := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 arithmetic: the auxiliary polynomial x^m*(x-1)^n is strictly increasing on real points greater than one. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: half_square_ge_self_of_ge_two
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem half_square_ge_self_of_ge_two
    {y : Real} (hy : 2 <= y) :
    y <= (1 / 2) * y ^ 2 := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 odd-case arithmetic: if y >= 2, then half of y squared dominates y. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: offset_ratio_ge_half_denominator_ratio
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem offset_ratio_ge_half_denominator_ratio
    {A B t : Real}
    (hA_nonneg : 0 <= A)
    (hB_pos : 0 < B)
    (ht_nonneg : 0 <= t)
    (ht_le_B : t <= B) :
    A / (2 * B) <= (A + t) / (B + t) := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 offset-ratio arithmetic: if the offset is at most the denominator value, then adding the offset loses at most a factor of two in the denominator. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: offset_ratio_ge_div_two_offset
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem offset_ratio_ge_div_two_offset
    {A B t : Real}
    (hA_nonneg : 0 <= A)
    (hB_nonneg : 0 <= B)
    (ht_pos : 0 < t)
    (hB_le_t : B <= t) :
    A / (2 * t) <= (A + t) / (B + t) := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 offset-ratio arithmetic: if the denominator value is at most the offset, bound the shifted ratio below by A/(2t). Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: two_mul_le_div_two_offset_of_offset_le_quarter
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem two_mul_le_div_two_offset_of_offset_le_quarter
    {A t : Real}
    (hA_nonneg : 0 <= A)
    (ht_pos : 0 < t)
    (ht_le_quarter : t <= 1 / 4) :
    2 * A <= A / (2 * t) := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 offset-ratio arithmetic: an offset at most 1/4 makes A/(2t) at least 2A. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: abs_belyi_aux_le_one_on_unit_interval
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem abs_belyi_aux_le_one_on_unit_interval
    {m n : Nat} {x : Real}
    (hm : 1 <= m)
    (hn : 1 <= n)
    (hx0 : 0 <= x)
    (hx1 : x <= 1) :
    |NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n x| <= 1 := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 arithmetic: on the unit interval, the absolute value of x^m*(x-1)^n is bounded by one. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: belyi_aux_beta_gt_unit_interval_value
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem belyi_aux_beta_gt_unit_interval_value
    {m n : Nat} {beta x : Real}
    (hm : 1 <= m)
    (hn : 1 <= n)
    (hbeta : 2 <= beta)
    (hx0 : 0 <= x)
    (hx1 : x <= 1) :
    NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n x <
      NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n beta := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 property (c) arithmetic: f(beta) is strictly larger than every auxiliary value on the unit interval when beta >= 2. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: belyi_aux_beta_ne_unit_interval_value
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem belyi_aux_beta_ne_unit_interval_value
    {m n : Nat} {beta x : Real}
    (hm : 1 <= m)
    (hn : 1 <= n)
    (hbeta : 2 <= beta)
    (hx0 : 0 <= x)
    (hx1 : x <= 1) :
    NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n beta ≠
      NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n x := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 property (c) arithmetic: f(beta) is not a value taken by the auxiliary polynomial on the unit interval when beta >= 2. Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: belyi_aux_beta_not_mem_image_of_finite_real_set
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Elementary

open Real

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem belyi_aux_beta_not_mem_image_of_finite_real_set
    {m n : Nat} {beta C : Real}
    (hm : 1 <= m)
    (hn : 1 <= n)
    (hC : 2 <= C)
    {S : Finset Real}
    (h_one_mem : 1 ∈ S)
    (hS_shape : ∀ x ∈ S, (0 <= x ∧ x <= 1) ∨ 1 < x)
    (hscale : ∀ x ∈ S, x ≠ 0 → beta / x >= C) :
    NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n beta ∉
      S.image (fun x => NoncriticalBelyi.belyiAux m n x) := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 property (c), finite affine form: under the scale condition and the paper's real-shape hypothesis on a finite set, f(beta) is not in the finite image f(S). Use the checked elementary theorem. -/

name: paperPolynomial_eval_zero
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Polynomial

open Polynomial

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem paperPolynomial_eval_zero
    {m n : Nat} (hm : 0 < m) :
    (NoncriticalBelyi.paperPolynomial m n).eval 0 = 0 := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 polynomial layer: X^m*(X-1)^n sends 0 to 0 when m is positive. Use the checked polynomial theorem. -/

name: paperPolynomial_eval_one
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Polynomial

open Polynomial

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem paperPolynomial_eval_one
    {m n : Nat} (hn : 0 < n) :
    (NoncriticalBelyi.paperPolynomial m n).eval 1 = 0 := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 polynomial layer: X^m*(X-1)^n sends 1 to 0 when n is positive. Use the checked polynomial theorem. -/

name: derivative_paperPolynomial
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Polynomial

open Polynomial

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem derivative_paperPolynomial
    (m n : Nat) :
    derivative (NoncriticalBelyi.paperPolynomial m n) =
      C (m : ℚ) * X ^ (m - 1) * (X - 1) ^ n +
        C (n : ℚ) * X ^ m * (X - 1) ^ (n - 1) := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 polynomial layer: formal derivative of X^m*(X-1)^n over Q[X]. Use the checked polynomial theorem. -/

name: derivative_paperPolynomial_factor
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Polynomial

open Polynomial

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem derivative_paperPolynomial_factor
    {m n : Nat} (hm : 0 < m) (hn : 0 < n) :
    derivative (NoncriticalBelyi.paperPolynomial m n) =
      X ^ (m - 1) * (X - 1) ^ (n - 1) *
        (C (((m + n : Nat) : ℚ)) * X - C (m : ℚ)) := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 polynomial layer: factor the derivative as X^(m-1)*(X-1)^(n-1)*((m+n)X-m). Use the checked polynomial theorem. -/

name: derivative_paperPolynomial_eval_middle_eq_zero
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Polynomial

open Polynomial

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem derivative_paperPolynomial_eval_middle_eq_zero
    {m n : Nat} (hm : 0 < m) (hn : 0 < n) :
    (derivative (NoncriticalBelyi.paperPolynomial m n)).eval
      ((m : ℚ) / (((m + n : Nat) : ℚ))) = 0 := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 polynomial layer: the middle point m/(m+n) is a zero of the formal derivative. Use the checked polynomial theorem. -/

name: paperPolynomial_basic_data
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Polynomial

open Polynomial

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem paperPolynomial_basic_data
    {m n : Nat} (hm : 0 < m) (hn : 0 < n) :
    (NoncriticalBelyi.paperPolynomial m n).eval 0 = 0 ∧
      (NoncriticalBelyi.paperPolynomial m n).eval 1 = 0 ∧
      derivative (NoncriticalBelyi.paperPolynomial m n) =
        X ^ (m - 1) * (X - 1) ^ (n - 1) *
          (C (((m + n : Nat) : ℚ)) * X - C (m : ℚ)) ∧
      (derivative (NoncriticalBelyi.paperPolynomial m n)).eval
        ((m : ℚ) / (((m + n : Nat) : ℚ))) = 0 := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 polynomial layer: package endpoint values, factored derivative, and the middle critical-point equation. Use the checked polynomial theorem. -/

name: derivative_paperPolynomial_eval_eq_zero_iff
header:
import HilbertTest.NoncriticalBelyi.Polynomial

open Polynomial

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem derivative_paperPolynomial_eval_eq_zero_iff
    {m n : Nat} (hm : 1 < m) (hn : 1 < n) (x : ℚ) :
    (derivative (NoncriticalBelyi.paperPolynomial m n)).eval x = 0 ↔
      x = 0 ∨ x = 1 ∨ x = (m : ℚ) / (((m + n : Nat) : ℚ)) := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 polynomial layer: when both endpoint exponents are at least two, the formal derivative vanishes exactly at 0, 1, and m/(m+n). Use the checked polynomial theorem. -/
